error_signature: producing 0 bytes when converting multiple images
title: 0 bytes output when converting multiple images from base64
symptom: When converting multiple images from base64 to binary in the 'Convert to File' node, the output files are all 0 bytes. However, converting 1-5 images works correctly.
applies_when: Converting more than 5 images at once
root_cause: The error occurs due to hitting memory limits or processing time constraints of the system when handling a large number of images simultaneously. This can lead to incomplete processing and result in 0-byte files.
fix_steps: Use the 'Loop Over Items' node before the 'Convert to File' node to process images in smaller batches (1-5 images).
Alternatively, increase Node.js memory limit by setting the `--max-old-space-size` option to 4 GB.
Consider switching to filesystem mode for binary data by setting `N8N_BINARY_DATA_MODE=filesystem` and defining a local storage path.
agent_instruction: Process images in smaller batches or adjust memory settings to avoid 0-byte outputs.
